preconditions: has at least 2 meta-information lines beyond ##fileformat
enforced.oracle: After shuffling all ##meta-information lines (keeping ##fileformat first), the parsed variant records must be identical to the original. Per VCF v4.5 §1.2, meta-information lines other than ##fileformat may appear in any order.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: fileformat must be first line — moving it breaks spec
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error or crash) when ##fileformat is not the first line; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality violation — extra value for biallelic record
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject an INFO field declared Number=A when the number of values does not equal the number of ALT alleles; silent truncation or acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.oracle: After parsing and re-serializing a VCF file through the SUT's writer API, all POS values, END INFO values, and REF allele strings must be identical to the original. This tests for off-by-one errors in coordinate handling during serialization.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: Missing-value FORMAT field injection round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: Appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing) to all samples is semantically a no-op. A spec-compliant parser must produce identical canonical content after round-tripping, as missing values are explicitly permitted in FORMAT fields.

evidence.quote: Note that the use of END_OF_VECTOR means that it is legal to encode a vector VCF field with MISSING values.

enforced.mr_name: POS and REF first-base coordinate consistency after meta-line shuffle
enforced.oracle: After shuffling meta-information lines (keeping ##fileformat first), the POS field of each record must still refer to the position of the first base of the REF allele. Shuffling header lines must not alter the relationship between POS and REF.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation preserves allele count and structural variant classification after writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles (with corresponding GT remapping and Number=A/R field reordering) and round-tripping through the SUT writer, query methods that return scalar properties of each record (e.g., getNAlleles, isStructural, getAltAlleles) must return values consistent with the original parsed record when accounting for the permutation. The number of alleles must remain the same; the set of alternate alleles must be the same up to permutation.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line reordering preserves header query methods after writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After shuffling meta-information lines (keeping ##fileformat pinned first) and round-tripping through the SUT writer, header query methods (e.g., getInfoKeys, getFormatKeys, getContigNames, getFilterIds, getSampleNames) must return the same sets of values as from the original parsed header.

evidence.quote: Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed. Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Structured key-value reordering preserves header field property queries after writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After reordering key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines and round-tripping through the SUT writer, header query methods that retrieve field properties (e.g., getNumber(ID), getType(ID), getDescription(ID)) must return identical values for every field ID.

evidence.quote: To aid human readability, the order of fields should be ID, Number, Type, Description, then any optional fields. Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffling preserves per-key INFO query methods after writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value entries in the INFO column and round-tripping through the SUT writer, query methods that retrieve INFO field values by key (e.g., getInfoField(key)) must return identical values for every key present in the original record.

evidence.quote: Possible Types for INFO fields are: Integer, Float, Flag, Character, and String. The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field.

enforced.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves per-sample genotype query methods after writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After permuting sample columns consistently across the entire file and round-tripping through the SUT writer, query methods that retrieve per-sample genotype data (e.g., getGT(sampleName), getSampleDepth(sampleName), getSampleAlleleIndices(sampleName)) must return the same values when mapped through the inverse permutation of sample names.

evidence.quote: If genotype data is present in the file, these are followed by a FORMAT column header, then an arbitrary number of sample IDs. Duplicate sample IDs are not allowed.
The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: BCF round-trip preserves all record-level query methods through writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: After round-tripping through BCF2 encoding (VCF -> BCF -> VCF) and then through the SUT writer, all public query methods on parsed records (e.g., getChrom, getPos, getRef, getAlt, getQual, getFilter, getInfoField, getFormatField) must return identical values. The BCF2 encoding must preserve all typed INFO and GENOTYPE fields per the spec requirement that they be fully typed in the header.

evidence.quote: All INFO and GENOTYPE fields must be fully typed in the BCF2 header to enable type-specific encoding of the fields in records. An error must be thrown when converting a VCF to BCF2 when an unknown or not fully specified field is encountered in the records.

BCF2 is a binary, compressed equivalent of VCF that can be indexed with tabix and can be efficiently decoded from disk or streams. For efficiency reasons BCF2 only supports a subset of VCF, in that all info and genotype fields must have their full types specified.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle INFO field key-value pairs preserves semantics
preconditions: at least one data record has multiple semicolon-separated INFO key=value pairs
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value entries in the INFO column, the parsed biological content must be identical. The INFO field is defined as a semicolon-separated series of key=value pairs with no ordering semantics specified.

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value `.' if none are present.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key order is semantically irrelevant, but it defines INFO as a 'series' without ordering constraints, and no known parser relies on key order.

quarantine.mr_name: Permute BCF header dictionary order preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER dictionary entries, round-tripping through BCF and back to VCF must produce identical canonical output. Per VCF v4.5 §6.2.1, BCF dictionary order is implementation-defined and parsers must not rely on implicit ordering.

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.

ambiguity_flags: The spec notes that BCF dictionary order matters for encoding but a compliant parser must handle any order via the IDX mechanism.

quarantine.mr_name: Permute sample column order preserves semantics
preconditions: at least 2 sample columns in the VCF header
quarantine.oracle: After permuting sample column order consistently across the header and all data lines, the parsed per-sample genotype data must be identical when matched by sample ID. Sample columns are identified by their column header IDs, not by positional index.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, but sample columns are identified by their header IDs and no ordering constraint is specified.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of Number=A, Number=R, Number=G fields and GT indices
quarantine.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping all dependent fields (GT allele indices, Number=A values per-ALT, Number=R values with REF first then reordered ALTs, Number=G values reordered per the genotype ordering scheme), the resulting VCF record must represent the same set of variants as the original. A spec-compliant parser must produce identical variant semantics.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column. G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section genotype-fields:genotype-ordering (see Genotype Ordering).
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
LAA		    .			    Integer	    1-based indices into ALT, indicating which alleles are relevant (local) for the current sample

Genotype Ordering. In general case of ploidy P and N alternate alleles (0 is the REF and 1… N the alternate alleles), the ordering of genotypes for the likelihoods can be expressed by the following pseudocode with as many nested loops as ploidy

quarantine.mr_name: Common affix trimming invariance (canonical normalization of REF/ALT)
quarantine.oracle: After trimming shared prefix/suffix bases between REF and ALT and adjusting POS accordingly, the variant must be recognized as the same biological variant. Per Tan et al. 2015 canonical normalization, a spec-compliant parser must treat the trimmed form as equivalent to the original.

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment or parsimonious representation; normalization is a convention from Tan et al. 2015. However, the spec requires POS to refer to the first base of REF, so trimming affixes with POS adjustment is a valid equivalent encoding.

quarantine.mr_name: Left-alignment of indels in homopolymer runs
quarantine.oracle: After left-shifting an indel in a homopolymer run (decrementing POS by 1, preserving first base of REF/ALT), the variant must be recognized as the same biological variant. A spec-compliant parser that normalizes must treat the left-aligned form as equivalent.

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment of indels in homopolymer runs; this is a normalization convention from Tan et al. 2015. The spec only requires POS to point to the first base of REF, so shifting POS while adjusting REF/ALT accordingly is a valid equivalent encoding.

quarantine.oracle: Round-tripping a VCF file through BCF encoding and back must produce semantically identical output. Per VCF v4.5 §6, BCF is a binary equivalent encoding; any difference in canonical output exposes a codec bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

ambiguity_flags: BCF encoding may handle missing values and empty vectors differently from VCF text representation (e.g., BCF encodes empty vectors as missing). This is a known implementation-defined behavior per VCF v4.5 §6.

quarantine.mr_name: missing mandatory fixed column — dropping QUAL from data row
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a data line with fewer than 8 fixed columns; silent acceptance or auto-filling with missing values is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The mandatory header line names the 8 fixed, mandatory columns. These columns are as follows: #CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO

quarantine.mr_name: GT allele index out of bounds — referencing non-existent ALT allele
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a GT field containing an allele index greater than the number of ALT alleles (len(ALT)); silent acceptance or coercion is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: For symbolic structural variant alleles, GT=0 indicates the absence of any of the ALT symbolic structural variants defined in the record.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state 'GT allele index must be <= len(ALT)' as a single normative sentence, but the semantics of GT=0 meaning REF and GT>0 referencing ALT alleles by position implies the constraint.

quarantine.mr_name: Permute structured header line key-value order preserves semantics
preconditions: header contains at least one ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER structured meta-information line with multiple key=value pairs
quarantine.oracle: After reordering key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER lines, the parsed variant records must be identical. Per VCF v4.5 §1.2, implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.mr_name: Multi-allelic split invariance (split multi-ALT record into single-ALT records)
quarantine.oracle: Splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele (with synchronized Number=A/R INFO/FORMAT arrays and remapped GT indices) must produce a semantically equivalent representation. A spec-compliant parser must recognize the split form as encoding the same variant set as the multi-ALT form.

evidence.quote: ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

quarantine.mr_name: BCF round-trip with permuted header dictionary preserves variant semantics
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and the BCF-round-tripped VCF (with permuted header dictionary order) must produce identical canonical variant records. Per VCF v4.5 §BCF specification, dictionary entries are indexed by offset; a spec-compliant parser must resolve values by key, not by position, so dictionary order permutation is a semantic no-op.

evidence.quote:
* All INFO and GENOTYPE fields must be fully typed in the BCF2 header to enable type-specific encoding of the fields in records.
  An error must be thrown when converting a VCF to BCF2 when an unknown or not fully specified field is encountered in the records.

Because the type is encoded directly in the header, the recommended extension for BCF2 formatted files is .bcf.
BCF2 supports encoding values in a dictionary of strings.
The string map is provided by the keyword  as a comma-separate ordered list of strings.
See the "Dictionary of strings" section for more details.

Note that the dictionary encoding has the magic prefix `s' here to indicate that the field's value is actually in the dictionary entry giving by the subsequent offset.
This representation isn't actually the one used in BCF2 records but it provides a clean visual guide for the above example.
Note also how the contig has been recoded as a offset into the list of contig declarations.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing value injection preserves existing genotype query methods after writer round-trip
quarantine.oracle: After appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing values) to all samples and round-tripping through the SUT writer, query methods that retrieve existing genotype fields (e.g., getGT, getDP, getGQ) must return identical values for all samples. The newly injected field must be queryable and return a missing/null sentinel for every sample.

evidence.quote: Zero length fields are not allowed, a dot (``.'') must be used instead.
If a call cannot be made for a sample at a given locus, `.' must be specified for each missing allele in the GT field (for example `./.' for a diploid genotype and `.' for haploid genotype).

ambiguity_flags: The spec allows '.' for missing values but does not mandate how parsers expose missing FORMAT fields through query APIs; implementations may differ in whether they return null, empty string, or a sentinel value.

quarantine.mr_name: Left-alignment of indels in homopolymer runs preserves variant identity queries after writer round-trip
quarantine.oracle: After left-aligning an indel in a homopolymer run (decrementing POS by 1, trimming first base from REF/ALT) and round-tripping through the SUT writer, query methods that retrieve the normalized variant representation (e.g., getRef, getAlt, getStart, getEnd) must reflect the left-shifted coordinates, while the variant's semantic identity (e.g., isIndel, getAlleleLengthDelta) must remain consistent with the original variant.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

ambiguity_flags: Left-alignment is a normalization convention (Tan 2015) rather than an explicit VCF spec requirement; parsers may differ in whether they normalize internally.
